data.title: An Observational Clinical Study to evalUate the Effectiveness of Bosentan Therapy in Patients With Systemic scleRosis and Progressive Digital ulcEr Disease.
data.brief_summary: Digital Ulcers (DEs), are painful open sores on the fingers and toes and are due to limited perfusion of blood vessels in patients with Scleroderma. In particular, ulcers are caused by narrowing of the arteries, resulting in reduced blood supply to the fingers, causing pain and difficult to heal leaving deep scars. DEs may be present on the rails or fingertips, on the extensor surfaces of the joints, and depending on the underlying calcification. The etiology of ulcers is multifactorial. Raynaud
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Adult patients
* Patients diagnosed with scleroderma (diffuse or limited)
* Patients with at least one digital ulcer, whether or not it is due to scleroderma
* Patients with 2013 ACR / EULAR Classification Criteria with a total score of ≥ 9
